en:
The Greenwood Boys are a famous pop group and they are traveling around the country.
Tomorrow they will arrive by train and young people will meet them at the station.
They will be giving five performances at the workers' club.
For this occasion the police will be trying to keep order all week.
Many fans will be waiting outside long before the first show begins.
By this time tomorrow evening, the whole town will be singing their songs.
cn:
格林伍德少年合唱团是一支著名流行乐队，正在全国巡演。
明天他们将乘火车到达，年轻人会去车站迎接。
他们将在工人俱乐部演出五场。
为此警察整周都将忙着维持秩序。
第一场开始前很久，许多歌迷就会在外面等候。
到明晚这个时候，全镇都将唱起他们的歌。

en:
Last year I drove across the south of France during my holiday.
As soon as I left a small village, a young man waved to me.
I had met him before, so I gave him a lift in my car.
I said a few words in French, but he hardly replied.
By the time we reached the town, we had spoken almost nothing.
Then he suddenly asked in English whether I spoke English.
cn:
去年假期我开车穿过法国南部。
我刚离开一个小村庄，一个年轻人就向我挥手。
我以前见过他，于是让他搭了我的车。
我用法语说了几句，但他几乎没回答。
等我们到镇上时，我们几乎一句话都没说。
这时他突然用英语问我会不会讲英语。

en:
My secretary told me that my boss wanted to see me at once.
I felt nervous because many workers had already left the firm.
When I entered his office, he said that business was very bad.
He added that the company could not afford such high salaries.
I thought he would dismiss me, but he interrupted me with a smile.
At last he said that my salary would be increased next month.
cn:
秘书告诉我老板要立刻见我。
我很紧张，因为已经有很多工人离开了公司。
我走进办公室时，他说生意很差。
他又补充说公司负担不起这么高的工资。
我以为他要辞退我，但他笑着打断了我。
最后他说下个月要给我加薪。

en:
If you park your car in the wrong place in this town, you usually get a ticket.
When I visited Sweden, I made the same mistake near a square.
I expected punishment, but I found only a polite note on my windscreen.
It said that if I paid attention to signs, I would enjoy my stay.
The note was just a friendly reminder and not a fine.
After that, I obeyed every traffic rule because kindness worked.
cn:
如果你在这个镇上把车停错地方，通常会收到罚单。
我去瑞典旅行时，也在广场附近犯了同样的错误。
我本以为会受罚，却只在挡风玻璃上发现一张礼貌便条。
便条上写着，如果我注意标志，就会有愉快的旅程。
这张纸只是善意提醒，不是罚款单。
从那以后我遵守每条交规，因为礼貌真的有效。

en:
My aunt is an actress and she appears on stage every night.
She must be over thirty-five, but she always plays young girls.
For each performance she wears bright dresses and long stockings.
I was invited to watch her latest play this evening.
Before I went, she warned me that I must not tell anyone her age.
She said she will have to stop acting if people know the truth.
cn:
我姑妈是一名演员，每晚都在舞台上登场。
她肯定已经超过三十五岁了，但总演少女角色。
每场演出她都穿鲜艳的裙子和长筒袜。
今晚我受邀去看她的新戏。
出门前她警告我，我绝对不能告诉别人她的年龄。
她说如果大家知道真相，她将不得不停止演出。

en:
After I had had lunch at a village pub, I looked for my bag.
I had left it beside the door, but it was gone.
I kept searching everywhere and could not find it.
The landlord came in, asked about my meal, and went out again.
A minute later he returned with my bag and smiled.
A dog had taken it into the garden, and he often does this.
cn:
我在乡村小酒馆吃过午饭后开始找包。
我本来把它放在门边，可它不见了。
我不停到处找，还是找不到。
店主走进来问我饭菜如何，又走了出去。
一分钟后他笑着拿着我的包回来了。
原来是一只狗把包叼进了花园，而且它经常这样。

en:
My friend and I hurried to the theatre and reached the ticket office late.
I asked whether we could buy two seats, but the clerk said they were sold out.
Just then a man arrived and said he might return two tickets.
The clerk accepted them and I exclaimed that we were lucky.
She replied sadly that those tickets were for next Wednesday.
My friend laughed and said we might as well buy them anyway.
cn:
我和朋友匆忙赶到剧院，晚了才到售票处。
我问能否买两张票，售票员说已经卖完了。
就在这时，一个男人赶来，说他可能要退两张票。
售票员收下后，我惊呼我们太幸运了。
她却遗憾地说那是下周三的票。
朋友笑着说那我们还是不妨买下来吧。

en:
Fishing is my favourite sport, but I am not a successful fisherman.
I often sit in a small boat all day and catch nothing.
Some friends advise me to give it up because it is a waste of time.
They cannot understand why I keep going to the river.
I realized that I am not really interested in catching fish.
I am only interested in sitting in a boat and doing nothing.
cn:
钓鱼是我最喜欢的运动，但我并不是成功的钓鱼人。
我常常整天坐在小船里，一条鱼也钓不到。
有些朋友劝我放弃，说这很浪费时间。
他们不明白我为什么还总去河边。
我意识到自己其实并不真正爱钓鱼。
我只是喜欢坐在船里，什么也不做。

en:
My house is near an airport, so planes pass over it all day.
The airport was built years ago, and many neighbours sold their houses.
People said that anyone who stayed here must be mad.
I was offered a large sum of money for my home.
I refused every offer because I was determined to stay.
Perhaps one day the noise will drive me mad, but I still remain.
cn:
我家靠近机场，所以飞机整天从屋顶飞过。
机场是几年前建成的，很多邻居都把房子卖了。
大家说留在这里的人一定疯了。
曾有人出很大一笔钱买我的房子。
我拒绝了所有报价，因为我决心留下。
也许有一天噪音会把我逼疯，但我现在仍然住着。

en:
My daughter Jane has just received a letter from a girl in Holland.
She never dreamed that this would happen.
Last year she threw a bottle into the Channel with her name and address.
Ten months later that girl found it and wrote back.
Now the two girls have become friends and write to each other regularly.
They have decided to use the post office because it is faster.
cn:
我女儿简刚收到一位荷兰女孩的来信。
她从没想过这件事会发生。
去年她把写有姓名和地址的瓶子扔进了海峡。
十个月后，那位女孩捡到瓶子并回了信。
现在两个女孩已经成了朋友，并定期通信。
她们已经决定改用邮局寄信，因为这样更快。

en:
Yesterday I received a letter from my sister who lives abroad.
She said she would return next year and visit us.
If she comes, she will be surprised by our new country house.
The work had begun before she left our city.
Now it was completed five months ago and we moved in happily.
I told her that she could finally see the house she had wanted.
cn:
昨天我收到了住在国外的姐姐来信。
她说她明年会回来并看望我们。
如果她回来，她会对我们的乡间新居感到惊讶。
在她离开这座城市前，工程就已经开始了。
房子在五个月前完工，我们高兴地搬了进去。
我告诉她，她终于可以看到一直想看的这栋房子了。

en:
I felt upset because fifty pounds had disappeared from my hotel room.
I went to the manager and told him that I had lost the money.
He was very sympathetic and said that people complained like this every day.
He added that the world was becoming a wicked place.
While he was speaking, a girl knocked and put an envelope on his desk.
The envelope contained my money, so things could have been much worse.
cn:
我很苦恼，因为旅馆房间里的五十英镑不见了。
我去找经理，告诉他我把钱弄丢了。
他很同情我，说每天都有人这样抱怨。
他还说这个世界正变得越来越坏。
他讲话时，一位姑娘敲门并把信封放在桌上。
信封里正是我的钱，所以事情本来还可能更糟。
